children.text: a permit for that vessel from the Secretary of Transportation; and
displaying a number or other marking on the vessel as prescribed by the Secretary under chapter 123 or .section 12502(b) of title 46
the name, address, and telephone number of the vessel owner and operator;
the vessel’s name and identification number;
the vessel’s area of operation;
the vessel’s transport capacity;

children.label: A
children.text: municipal waste;

children.label: B
children.text: commercial waste;

children.label: C
children.text: medical waste; or

children.label: D
children.text: waste of another character.
any other information the Secretary may require; and
an acknowledgment.
is effective 30 days after the date on which it was issued;
may be issued only for a period of not more than 5 years after the effective date of the permit;
may be renewed for periods of not more than 5 years only by the vessel owner or operator that applied for the original permit; and
is terminated when the vessel is sold.

notify the applicant of the denial and the reasons for the denial; and
provide an opportunity for a hearing on the denial.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The permit issued for a vessel under this chapter shall be maintained in a manner prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Endorsements
children.label: 2 Endorsements
children.text: If a vessel is a documented vessel, the Secretary may endorse a permit on the vessel’s certificate of documentation.

children.heading: Vessel information system
children.label: g Vessel information system
children.text: The Secretary may include information in a permit in the vessel information system maintained under chapter 125 of title 46.
